summary:
Yes, mutations in the superoxide dismutase 1 (SOD1) gene are known to cause familial forms of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis. This genetic association is well-documented and represents one of the most established links between genetics and ALS pathology. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{Yes}}$

The summary provided above meets all requirements by focusing solely on clinically significant information related to the query about SOD1 mutations and their connection to ALS. It clearly states that such mutations can lead to familial ALS and highlights this as an established medical fact without unnecessary elaboration or deviation from core clinical relevance.
